description: Sequential row index of the tweet in the original dataset.
Unique Twitter/X tweet identifier. Can be used with the Twitter/X API to rehydrate (retrieve) the original tweet content.
Name of the brand/company mentioned in the tweet. Tweets in this file are those from the reference corpus used to identify the 39 studied companies.
values_range_or_example: One of 39 companies: Adidas; Amazon; Audi; BMW; Budweiser; Coach; Coca-Cola; Colgate-Palmolive; Costco; Facebook; FedEx; Ford; General Electric; Google; Gucci; H&M; Hewlett-Packard; Honda; IBM; Intel; Louis Vuitton; Mcdonalds; Michelin; Microsoft; Nike; Pampers; Pepsi; Samsung; Shell; Sony; Starbucks; UPS; Vauxhall; Verizon Communications; Volkswagen; Walmart; Walt Disney Company; Wells Fargo; eBay

description: Anonymous pseudonymous user identifier. Real Twitter handles have been replaced with a consistent U_<number> code. Users in U_1 to U_4776 also appear in users_profile_data.csv. Users U_4777 and above appear only in this file (they tweeted about a company but were not in the tracked user cohort).
values_range_or_example: U_1 to U_8619 (U_1–U_4776: in profile data; U_4777–U_8619: reference-only users)

description: Anonymous pseudonymous user identifier corresponding to one of the 4776 tracked Twitter users. Matches the user_id column in reference_tweet_data.txt where applicable. Row order matches the row index of the binary activity matrices (mentions_dataset; no_mentions_dataset; no_tweet_dataset) for the 3875-user analysis subset.
Number of tweets the user had liked (favorited) at the time of data collection.
Number of followers the user had at the time of data collection.
Number of accounts the user was following at the time of data collection.
Primary language of the user account as reported by Twitter/X at the time of data collection.
values_range_or_example: e.g. en; en-gb; fr; de; ar; ja; etc.

description: Number of public Twitter/X lists the user appeared on at the time of data collection.
Total number of tweets (statuses) the user had posted at the time of data collection.
Whether the user account held a verified badge on Twitter/X at the time of data collection.
Zero-based row index identifying each user. Row order corresponds to the 3875-user analysis subset derived from users_profile_data.csv.
Each column header is a UTC datetime string representing the start of a 20-minute time bin. There are 17566 time bins covering 2016-07-14 03:00:00 to 2017-03-15 02:00:00.
Binary indicator: 1.0 if the user posted at least one tweet mentioning another account (@mention) during this 20-minute bin; 0.0 otherwise.
Zero-based row index. Same user ordering as mentions_dataset.csv.
Same 17566 time bins as mentions_dataset.csv.
Binary indicator: 1.0 if the user posted at least one tweet without an @mention during this 20-minute bin; 0.0 otherwise.
Zero-based row index. Same user ordering as mentions_dataset.csv.
Same 17566 time bins as mentions_dataset.csv.

Binary indicator: 1.0 if the user posted no tweets of any kind during this 20-minute bin; 0.0 otherwise. Note: for each user and time bin exactly one of mentions_dataset; no_mentions_dataset; and no_tweet_dataset equals 1.0.
One-based row index (generated by R's write.csv). Corresponds to the same user ordering as the binary activity matrices.

Cluster assignment produced by the categorical functional data clustering method. Values are 0 (noise/unassigned) or 1–3 (cluster membership). Cluster 0: 19 users; Cluster 1: 3284 users; Cluster 2: 541 users; Cluster 3: 31 users.

Pre-computed multinomial GAM estimation and FPCA results used to generate all Twitter application figures. Loaded by code/twitter_application/Twitter_DataAnalysis_Final.R. Contains: p1_figure_final; p2_figure_final; p3_figure_final (each 2000 x 3830): estimated category probability curves evaluated at 2000 time points for 3830 users; Z1_figure; Z2_figure (each 2000 x 3830): estimated linear predictor curves; vec0 (14 users: noise cluster); vec1 (3732 users: Cluster 1); vec2 (84 users: Cluster 2); tclusterdata (3830 x 5 data frame with FPC scores and cluster labels); argval (numeric vector length 2000: evaluation grid); timestamps01 (numeric vector length 2000: normalised time in [0;1]); p_min1; p_max1; p_min3; p_max3; z_min1; z_max1; which_columnsp3.

3861 x 2000 binary matrix of raw categorical states for the user-time combinations used in estimation. Rows correspond to users; columns correspond to the 2000 time points. Optional input for individual trajectory plots (see commented block in Twitter_DataAnalysis_Final.R).
